note: Reverses the fix for GHSA-5fw2-8jcv-xh87 / CVE-2026-... (Critical, CVSS 9.8), an account-takeover via operator injection in Parse Server's authentication-data lookup. Parse Server (parse-community, 21k+ stars) is a widely deployed Express/Node backend-as-a-service. In Auth.js findUsersWithAuthData, each third-party provider's authData.id is used to build a query: { ['authData.<provider>.id']: providerAuthData.id }. The fix added a guard requiring providerAuthData.id to be a string; without it, an attacker can submit an OBJECT as the id (e.g. a query operator like {"$regex": ".*"} or {"$ne": null}) which is injected straight into the query, matching another user's auth record and logging the attacker in as that user (account takeover). This suite removes that 4-line type-check guard. Diff-visible: the reviewer sees a value that flows into a database query lose its 'must be a string' validation, while the surrounding code still treats it as a trusted id.

bugs.owasp: A01: Broken Access Control / A03: Injection (NoSQL operator injection -> authentication bypass / account takeover)
bugs.severity_note: GHSA-5fw2-8jcv-xh87 is rated Critical (CVSS 9.8): unauthenticated account takeover via operator injection in the auth-data identifier. Mapped to the board's high, its top tier, not inflated.
bugs.description: Reverts the fix for GHSA-5fw2-8jcv-xh87. In findUsersWithAuthData (src/Auth.js), each provider's authData.id is interpolated into a query object: { ['authData.<provider>.id']: providerAuthData.id }. The fix added a guard that throws unless providerAuthData.id is a string. This regression removes that guard, so a client can pass an OBJECT as the id, e.g. {"$ne": null} or {"$regex": "^"} or {"$gt": ""}, which Parse/Mongo interprets as a query OPERATOR rather than a literal id. The injected operator matches another user's auth record (or any user), so the attacker authenticates as a victim, an unauthenticated account takeover. The catch: a reviewer must see that providerAuthData.id is placed into a database query without a type check, and that allowing a non-string (an object) enables NoSQL operator injection / auth bypass. Flagging a generic 'missing validation' is partial; the catch names operator/NoSQL injection or account-takeover/auth-bypass via a non-string id reaching the query.

detection_hints._note: A catch must name the SECURITY IMPACT, operator/NoSQL injection or auth-bypass/account-takeover, not merely 'a type check was removed' (that's a diff restatement, which the methodology counts as partial, not a catch).
